name: Data Pipeline Test Suite — Caching Layer
scenario: Add production-grade Caffeine caching to the Flipkart-style product import pipeline. Product data is read far more often than written — perfect for caching. The cache layer eliminates database load for read-heavy workloads while keeping data consistent with targeted cache eviction.
description: Add @Cacheable, @CachePut, @CacheEvict to the ProductService from Module 13. Configure Caffeine with per-cache TTLs. Write @SpringBootTest tests verifying cache hits, evictions, and consistency.

core.description: @EnableCaching on Application class. `CacheConfig` @Configuration with Caffeine: 'products' cache (max=1000, 5min TTL, recordStats), 'products-by-category' cache (max=200, 5min TTL).
@SpringBootTest tests (minimum 4): findAll caches result; findById caches per id; save evicts list cache; delete evicts both product and list cache.
Performance test: call findAll() 1000 times in a loop, assert total time < 1 second (proves cache eliminates DB overhead).

bonus.description: Add @Cacheable conditional: findByCategory only caches results with >= 2 products (using condition='#result != null && #result.size() >= 2'). Test that single-product categories are not cached.
Add cache statistics endpoint: GET /api/admin/cache-stats returns {cacheName, hitCount, missCount, hitRate} for each Caffeine cache (use cache.getNativeCache().stats()).
Add @Scheduled(fixedRate=300000) job that pre-warms the cache by calling findAll() and findByCategory for all known categories — ensures cache is warm after TTL expiry.

data_to_use:
Seed data (5 products):
- Wireless Mouse (Electronics), ₹799
- Keyboard (Electronics), ₹1999
- USB-C Hub (Accessories), ₹499
- Monitor (Electronics), ₹18999
- Ergonomic Chair (Furniture), ₹8999

Cache config:
- 'products': max=1000, TTL=5min
- 'products-by-category': max=200, TTL=5min
- recordStats=true on both
example_output:
# First findAll():
DB hit: findAll() — fetched 5 products

# Second findAll() (500ms later):
[cache hit] — 5 products, no DB query

# save(new product):
DB write: save Headphones
[cache evicted: products, products-by-category]

# findAll() after save:
DB hit: findAll() — fetched 6 products (cache was cleared)

# Performance test:
1000 calls to findAll(): 45ms total (0.045ms avg — cache!)

# Test results:
✓ findAll_populatesProductsCache
✓ findById_cachesPerProductId
✓ save_evictsAllListCaches
✓ delete_evictsProductAndListCaches
✓ performanceTest_1000callsUnder1Second
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Key patterns: @Caching(evict={...}, put={...}) for multiple cache operations. Cache test: @BeforeEach clear all caches + reload test data. Performance: long before=System.nanoTime(); for(int i=0;i<1000;i++) service.findAll(); assertTrue((System.nanoTime()-before)/1_000_000 < 1000). SimpleKey.EMPTY for no-arg method cache check.
submission_instructions: Push to GitHub. All 5 tests pass. Add cache TTL and maxSize comments explaining why those values were chosen.
tips_for_success: @Caching for save: 3 annotations in one — put product, evict products list, evict category list
@BeforeEach: clear both caches AND reload data — essential for isolation
Performance test: call findAll 1000x, measure total nanoseconds, convert to ms
CacheConfig: use registerCustomCache for per-cache TTL control
concepts_reinforced: Caffeine CacheManager with TTL and maxSize
Cache performance: verify near-zero latency on repeated calls
